message:
fix(packs): close global-flags bypass in dns/cloudflare, loadbalancer/traefik, system/disk

Extends the same bypass-fix already landed in commits 5293bcf and
c367e62 to the remaining three packs that still had the naive
`<tool>\s+<subcommand>` pattern shape. Those adjacency-only patterns
missed invocations that insert a global flag between the tool name
and the subcommand, e.g.:

  terraform -chdir=./infra destroy -target=cloudflare_record.x   (MISSED)
  docker --context=prod stop traefik                             (MISSED)
  kubectl -n prod delete pod traefik-xxxx                        (MISSED)
  btrfs --quiet subvolume delete /mnt/data/@snap                 (MISSED)
  dmsetup --noheadings remove cryptroot                          (MISSED)

Rewrites each affected pattern from
  r"<cli>\s+<subcommand>"
to
  r"<cli>\b.*?\s+<subcommand>"

The `\b.*?` allows any trailing-flags text (non-greedy) but still
anchors the CLI token on a word boundary, so plain `terraformer
destroy` and similar prefix-collision CLIs are not accidentally
matched. `.*?` is minimal so the pattern still reaches the intended
subcommand keyword without inflating match backtracking.

Coverage:

src/packs/dns/cloudflare.rs
  - cloudflare-terraform-destroy-record (terraform destroy -target=
    cloudflare_record.*)

src/packs/loadbalancer/traefik.rs
  Safe patterns:
  - docker-traefik-inspect (inspect/logs on a traefik container)
  - kubectl-traefik-get (get/describe on an ingressroute)
  Destructive patterns:
  - traefik-docker-stop (docker stop/kill traefik container)
  - traefik-docker-rm (docker rm traefik container)
  - traefik-kubectl-delete-pod (kubectl delete pod/deployment/
    daemonset traefik)
  - traefik-kubectl-delete-ingressroute (kubectl delete ingressroute)

src/packs/system/disk.rs
  Safe patterns (read-only btrfs/dmsetup introspection):
  - btrfs-subvolume-list, btrfs-subvolume-show,
    btrfs-filesystem-show, btrfs-filesystem-df,
    btrfs-filesystem-usage, btrfs-device-stats,
    btrfs-property-get, btrfs-scrub-status,
    dmsetup-ls, dmsetup-status, dmsetup-info, dmsetup-table,
    dmsetup-deps
  Destructive patterns (btrfs/dmsetup mutating commands, unchanged
  list but same rewrite applied uniformly).

No pattern was relaxed semantically — every previously-matched
command still matches. The set of commands the patterns catch is
now strictly larger by exactly the global-flag-variant forms.

files.patch:
@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@ fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
         ),
         destructive_pattern!(
             "cloudflare-terraform-destroy-record",
-            r"terraform\s+destroy\s+.*-target=(?:resource\.)?cloudflare_record\.",
+            r"terraform\b.*?\s+destroy\s+.*-target=(?:resource\.)?cloudflare_record\.",
             "terraform destroy -target=cloudflare_record deletes specific DNS records.",
             High,
             "Terraform destroy removes DNS records from Cloudflare. While Terraform state \

@@ -43,12 +43,12 @@ fn create_safe_patterns() -> Vec<SafePattern> {

             "docker-traefik-inspect",
-            r"docker\s+(?:inspect|logs)\s+.*\btraefik\b"
+            r"docker\b.*?\s+(?:inspect|logs)\s+.*\btraefik\b"

         safe_pattern!(
             "kubectl-traefik-get",
-            r"kubectl\s+(?:get|describe)\s+.*\bingressroute"
+            r"kubectl\b.*?\s+(?:get|describe)\s+.*\bingressroute"
         ),
     ]
